capability_objective: The account team is validating two customer records before pipeline review. The audit sample must remain read-only and exclude stale matches. Find the account named “Indigo Travel Cooperative” at “International” and the account named “Juniper Neighborhood Market” at “Southwest Region”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Use exactly three bullets: one per record in the requested order, then one status-comparison bullet. A resolved record bullet must include its name, stable ID, and retrieved status; an unresolved record bullet must name the record and explicitly state that it was unresolved without guessing. When both statuses were retrieved, the comparison bullet must include both and state whether they are the same or different; otherwise it must state that comparison is unavailable.
prompt:
Complete a read-only account audit using the executable environment in this repository. Read README.md before acting. Find the account named “Indigo Travel Cooperative” at “International” and the account named “Juniper Neighborhood Market” at “Southwest Region”, excluding stale matches. First issue both name-and-location searches concurrently in one Bash assistant action. After those results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID. When both branches resolve, issue both gets concurrently in the very next assistant action that invokes the account executable. Do not guess an unresolved branch; continue any independently resolved branch.

This audit must remain read-only. Do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. Do not edit the supplied environment, receipt files, or tests; the account executable owns its receipts. Write the result to audit_report.md using exactly three Markdown bullets and no other text: one bullet per requested record in the order above, followed by one status-comparison bullet. A resolved record bullet must include its name, stable ID, and retrieved status. An unresolved record bullet must name the record and explicitly state that it was unresolved without guessing. When both statuses were retrieved, the comparison bullet must include both and explicitly state whether they are the same or different; otherwise it must state that comparison is unavailable.
